{"step_by_step_thinking": "The inter-radicular area refers to the region between the roots of teeth. Cortical bone is dense and forms the outer layer of bones, providing protection and support. Cancellous bone, also known as trabecular or spongy bone, is found in the interior of bones and provides support with less density. Osteophytic bone refers to bone spurs, and exophytic bone refers to bone that grows outward. Given the location of the inter-radicular area, the dense outer layer of the tooth socket would be composed of cortical bone.", "answer_choice": "A"}
